rejected.title: AACR 2026:多款国产FIC新药将首次登场
rejected.snippet:
美国癌症研究协会(AACR)年会是全球肿瘤学领域规模最大、最具影响力的学术盛会之一,2026年AACR年会将于2026年4月17日至22日在美国圣地亚哥举行。

目前,AACR年会已成为国内创新药企展现最新研究成果的重要平台。在今年会议上,有超过100家中国药企亮相,带来近400项研究成果,覆盖ADC、双抗、多抗、mRNA、AI制药等前沿赛道。

百奥赛图将以海报形式展示36项最新研究成果,全人单克隆抗体、双/多特异性抗体、单抗及双特异性抗体偶联药物(ADC)、纳米抗体(VHH)及TCR分子等多种药物形式,以及12项临床前动物模型与药理药效研究进展。

维立志博将在会上公布两项临床前管线的最新研究成果,展现IO+ADC协同平台技术实力。具体为:全球首创T细胞衔接器-药物偶联物(TDC)LBL-054和新型的EGFR/PD-L1双抗ADC药物LBL-061的临床前研究数据。

橙帆医药将有三款创新药物亮相本次大会,分别为临床阶段的VBC101(EGFR/cMET双抗ADC)和临床前阶段的VBC229(DLL3双表位TCE-TOPO1i融合分子)、VTS208(CD3/CLDN18.2/CDH17三特异性TCE)。

君实生物将首次公布JS212(EGFR/HER3双抗ADC)用于晚期实体瘤的一项I期首次人体(FIH)临床研究及其一项临床前研究数据,JS207(PD-1/VEGF双抗)联合化疗用于转移性结直肠癌(CRC)、JS207(CTLA-4单抗)联合JS007用于晚期肝细胞癌(HCC)的两项Ⅱ期临床研究数据。

rejected.title: AMD宣布“Advancing AI 2026”大会
rejected.snippet:
新闻要点:

·Advancing AI是AMD全球旗舰AI大会,汇聚众多开发者、客户、企业领导者和合作伙伴,展示AMD AI解决方案的最新进展。

2026年4月28日,加利福尼亚州圣克拉拉讯 –AMD(超威,纳斯达克股票代码:AMD)已宣布其全球旗舰AI大会“Advancing AI”将于2026年7月23日在旧金山莫斯康展览中心(Moscone Center)通过线下和线上直播方式举办。本次大会将为AI开放生态系统带来由AMD驱动的AI在构建、部署和扩展方面的蓝图。

董事会主席及首席执行官苏姿丰博士(Dr. Lisa Su)将与众多AMD领导者,携手AI生态系统合作伙伴、客户和开发者共同探讨AMD从芯片到软件的端到端AI解决方案正在如何重塑AI与高性能计算的格局。

本次大会将于7月23日星期四在现场举办,并通过AMD官方频道进行直播。

rejected.title: 1条 git push 命令触发 GitHub 严重漏洞
rejected.snippet:
IT之家 4 月 29 日消息,安全机构 Wiz Research 昨日(4 月 28 日)发布博文,披露 GitHub 存在严重漏洞 CVE-2026-3854。攻击者仅需一条标准 git push 命令,即可触发远程代码执行,进而访问数百万公共和私有仓库。

该漏洞追踪编号为 CVE-2026-3854,任何经过身份验证的用户只需执行标准的 git push 命令,就能在 GitHub 后端服务器上执行任意代码。

GitHub 远程代码执行漏洞 CVE-2026-3854

该漏洞源于 GitHub 内部 X-Stat 标头的注入缺陷。X-Stat 是一个用分号分隔的协议,负责在内部服务间传递安全元数据。

当用户运行带有选项的 git push 命令后,GitHub 的 babeld 代理会直接将用户提供的字符串嵌入 X-Stat 标头,且未过滤分号。

由于标头解析器采用“最后写入生效”逻辑,攻击者只需注入分号和字段名,就能悄悄覆盖服务器已设定的安全配置。研究员将三个注入字段串联,成功实现完整的远程代码执行。

results.title: 肌肉男更聪明,也更深情?!两项研究:从基因表型层面证实男性肌肉力量、身体形态与智力的正向关联;抗阻训练能实实在在延缓大脑衰老
results.snippet:
“四肢发达,头脑简单”,这句流传了几十年的老话,至今仍是贴在肌肉发达男性身上撕不掉的刻板标签。更有许多人,默认身材健硕、力量感强的男性,不仅智商平平,在感情里也多半是爱表现自己、花心滥情的类型。

前不久,我们发布了一篇关于「肌肉」与「智商」的文章,其中丹麦科学家团队的随机对照试验给出了颠覆认知的结论:俯卧撑、撸铁、深蹲这类抗阻训练,不仅能练出结实的肌肉、强化骨骼与体能,更能系统性延缓大脑衰老。研究通过 AI「脑年龄时钟」模型验证,坚持 2 年中等强度力量训练的人群,大脑年龄比实际年龄年轻了 2.26 岁!

文章发布后,很多读者在后台留言提问:后天练肌肉能给大脑“保鲜抗衰”,那先天的肌肉力量、身体形态,和智力之间,是不是本来就存在着深层的科学关联?

2026 年 2 月正式见刊于《进化心理科学》(Evolutionary Psychological Science),由美国奥克兰大学、维也纳大学联合完成的这项重磅研究,就从基因表型的根源层面,彻底打破了流传多年的刻板偏见。这项研究不仅证实了年轻男性的肌肉力量、身体形态与智力之间存在显著的正向关联,更意外发现了智力、身体特征与性滥交行为之间的特殊关系,给了「肌肉男更花心」的偏见一记响亮的耳光!

results.title: 2026年,论文降重高效新法揭秘
results.snippet:
又到一年毕业季,无数研究生、博士生正对着查重报告“头秃”。重复率居高不下,不仅影响毕业,更关乎学术诚信。传统的“同义词替换”“语序调整”早已捉襟见肘,甚至可能扭曲原意。2026年的今天,一种融合了前沿人工智能技术的高效降重新范式正在兴起,它不再是简单的文字游戏,而是基于深度理解的智能重构与创新辅助。

一、传统降重之痛:效率低下与语义失真

根据中国知网等平台的公开数据,近年来高校毕业论文的平均查重率要求日趋严格,部分顶尖院校的硕士、博士论文要求重复率低于5%。面对动辄数万甚至十万字的论文,手动降重耗时巨大。更严重的是,生硬的改写常常导致核心概念表述不清、逻辑链条断裂,损害了论文的学术价值。

案例佐证:某高校理工科博士生在修改文献综述部分时,为降低重复率,将一系列专业术语进行了替换,导致评审专家在盲审中认为其对该领域核心概念理解有偏差,论文被要求重大修改,延期毕业半年。

Stay tuned! - __[2026/03]__ SCRL paper was released on arXiv! ## π Overview We propose SCRL (Selective-Complementary Reinforcement Learning), a robust test-time reinforcement learning framework that effectively mitigates label noise amplification. SCRL develops Selective Positive Pseudo-Labeling, which enforces strict consensus criteria to filter unreliable majorities. Complementarily, SCRL introduces Entropy-Gated Negative Pseudo-Labeling, the first negative supervision mechanism in TTRL, to reliably prune incorrect trajectories based on generation uncertainty. Extensive experiments on multiple reasoning benchmarks demonstrate that SCRL achieves substantial improvements over baselines, while maintaining robust generalization and training stability under constrained rollout budgets. ![Image 1: Framework of SCRL](https://github.com/Jasper-Yan/SCRL/raw/main/figs/overview.png) ## β‘οΈ Getting Started ### Environment Setup ```

readers.readerExcerpt: ## From Past To Path: Masked History Learning for Next-Item Prediction in Generative Recommendation __ACL 2026 Main Conference__ This is the official repository for __MHL__, accepted to __ACL 2026 Main Conference__. MHL is a novel training framework for generative recommendation that shifts the objective from simple next-step prediction to deep comprehension of history. It augments the standard autoregressive objective with an auxiliary task of reconstructing masked historical items, compelling the model to understand "why" an item path is formed from the user's past behaviors. The framework features an __entropy-guided masking__ policy that intelligently targets the most informative historical items, and a __curriculum learning__ scheduler that progressively transitions from history reconstruction to future prediction. ![Image 1: MHL Method](https://github.com/CQU-MM-Intelligent-Lab/MHL/raw/main/asset/method.png) --- ### Quick Setup Create and activate a new conda environment named `MHL_env`: ``` conda create -n MHL_env python=3.10 -y && conda activate MHL_env && pip install torch==2.6.0 --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu124 && pip install -r requirements.txt ``` --- ## Repository Structure ``` genrec/ ├── pipeline.py # Training/evaluation orchestration ├── trainer.py # Training logic with curriculum learning scheduler ├── model.py # Abstract model base class ├──

readers.readerExcerpt: ## LLM-Augmented Visual Representation Learning Official PyTorch implementation for paper _Multi-Modal Large Language Models are Effective Vision Learners_, accepted by _WACV 2025_. ### [Paper] [Supplementary Material] ![Image 1](https://github.com/lisun-ai/LMVR/raw/main/figures/main.png) ### Abstract Large language models (LLMs), pre-trained on vast amounts of text, have shown remarkable abilities in understanding general knowledge and commonsense. Therefore, it's desirable to leverage pre-trained LLM for help solve computer vision tasks. Previous works on multi-modal LLM mainly focus on the generation capability. In this work, we propose LLM-augmented visual representation learning (LMVR). Our approach involves initially using a vision encoder to extract features, which are then projected into the word embedding space of the LLM. The LLM then generates responses based on the visual representation and a text prompt. Finally, we aggregate sequence-level features from the hidden layers of the LLM to obtain image-level representations. readers.readerTitle: GitHub - facebookresearch/MetaEmbed: [ICLR 2026 Oral] Official Implementation of the paper "MetaEmbed: Scaling Multimodal Retrieval at Test-Time with Flexible Late Interactions" · GitHub
readers.readerExcerpt: MetaEmbed is a framework for multimodal retrieval that rethinks how multimodal embeddings are constructed and interacted with at scale. During training, a fixed number of learnable __Meta Tokens__ are appended to the input sequence. At test-time, their last-layer contextualized representations serve as compact yet expressive __multi-vector embeddings__. Through the proposed __Matryoshka Multi-Vector Retrieval (MMR)__ training, MetaEmbed learns to organize information by granularity across multiple vectors, enabling test-time scaling where users can balance retrieval quality against efficiency by selecting the number of tokens used for indexing and retrieval. - Paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.18095 ## Matryoshka Multi-Vector Retrieval (MMR) Standard multi-vector retrieval uses a fixed number of embedding vectors per query/document, offering no flexibility to trade off quality vs. efficiency at test time. MetaEmbed addresses this with __Matryoshka Multi-Vector Retrieval (MMR)__, a training strategy inspired by Matryoshka Representation Learning but applied at the _token level_ across multiple vectors. During training, MMR jointly optimizes late-interaction retrieval losses at __multiple granularities__ of the multi-vector representation. Specifically, the loss is computed over several `(num_query_tokens, num_doc_tokens)` groups simultaneously, e.g., `(1,1), (2,4), (4,8), (8,16)

A Teacher–Student Cooperation Framework to Synthesize Student-Consistent SFT Data _On-policy SFT Data Synthesis for Reasoning Models_ (__🎉🎉🎉 Accepted By ICML 2026__) | ![Image 2: Shailab InternLM Logo](https://github.com/CoopReason/TESSY/raw/main/figs/logo_internlm.png) 📄 Paper Link | 🤗 Training Set for Code Generation --- ## 🚀 Motivation Training reasoning models (e.g., Qwen3) is highly sensitive to the data distribution. We observe that: > ❗ Using off-policy data (e.g., directly from a strong teacher model) for SFT can lead to __severe catastrophic forgetting__, especially for complex reasoning tasks. --- ## 💡 Key Idea To address this critical issue, we propose __TESSY__, a novel __Teacher–Student Cooperative Data Synthesis framework__ designed to generate _on-policy_ training data. Instead of relying on a teacher model to fully generate training samples, TESSY __decouples the generation process into two distinct parts__: - 🧠 __Teacher model__ → specializes in generating _capability tokens_. - ✍️ __Student model__ → focuses on generating _style tokens_ (e.g., Hmm, Wait...).
